programmes.show_description: The Goggleboxers settle in for Celebrity Bake Off, The Dog House, Saturday Night Live UK, Britain's Got Talent, Celebrity Sabotage, BBC News on Easter eggs and more.
(2021) Wes Anderson's star-encrusted tribute to the New Yorker magazine covers the final edition of a chaotic fictional publication, following the death of its editor - Bill Murray.
Jessica Fostekew and Joe Lycett enjoy a Canarian caper, as they trike through Lanzarote's lunar landscape, sample volcanic vino and cactus croquettes, and tour the lava tunnels.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Natalie Palmer flies solo as she hosts at The Oak pub and B&B in Charing, Kent. Her guests admire her work ethic, but they're not blown away by the extractor fans.
Dennis Gilligan hosts at The White Lady in Worstead, Norfolk. Will his rivals encounter the legendary ghostly guest?
Former couple Katherine and Stewart offer an alternative spiritual experience at Cranleigh House B&B in Devon. But Dennis and Gary struggle to keep a straight face during a hilltop healing.
The last visit is to Paignton in Devon, where Mike and Denise are the new kids on the block, having only opened the doors to their B&B, Beaches, a few weeks earlier.
It's the final day of the competition and tensions run high as the B&Bers meet for the final time to air their grievances, before the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Shauna and Sam's Lancashire glampsite boasts a wealth of top-end facilities, including hot tubs, cinema screens and pizza ovens. Is it enough to see them victorious?
At luxury self-catering digs in Bradford, the B&Bers aren't sure that flat-pack furniture quite equates to the promised opulence.
At a quirky hotel in the centre of York that features a dungeon room full of chains, the B&Bers indulge in some BDSM: bellyaching, disagreeing, stain-hunting and moaning.
Martial arts expert Dan hosts the week's final visit in Southport, where he aims to please with his no-nonsense approach to a classic seaside getaway.
Allegations of game playing are rife on payment day, with one pair vastly underpaying everyone. You could cut the tension with a best-value-B&B plaque!

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Herefordshire, Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo meet George, a collector for over 70 years. Max finds a crate of vintage motor oil bottles that could be worth a small fortune.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In the Devon countryside, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet collectors Derek and Daphne. Henry finds a very rare tractor, while Simon discovers a Raleigh Shopper bike.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo meet retired haulier Jim in Worcestershire. Max wants to transform old air tanks into a sports car, while Simon turns some barrels into a bar bench.
Angie and Alan want to fulfil their dream of buying their very first property together on Spain's beautiful Orihuela Costa. Can Jasmine Harman find the perfect place for their £60k?
Bev and Errol can't wait to buy a Spanish holiday home in Mar Menor. Jasmine Harman shows them what their £65k can buy.
Nat and Dave are ready to buy a holiday home on Lanzarote, a place they visit twice a year. Jean tries to find their perfect property on the island they love so much.
Corina and Honey the cavapoo get off to a shaky start, but Honey has a trick or two that might just turn things around. And is Ruby the perfect teammate for football-mad Josh?

(2022) Sci-fi disaster with Halle Berry. A NASA bigwig, a disgraced astronaut and a conspiracy theorist are all that stand between the Earth and a plummeting Moon. But are aliens involved?
(2022) Don't-go-down-into-the-cellar horror with Georgina Campbell and Bill Skarsgård. A woman enters some very dark places below the double-booked Airbnb that she's staying in.
The brokers at UK Sotheby's International Realty secure more multi-million-pound sales. Can Lee find a buyer for a £29m house overlooking Regents Park lake?
Heather, who's 65, is rushed to Queen's after collapsing in the park. Long-serving member of the team Deanna speaks of her previous struggles with addiction.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien restore a metal workbench, a pine chest and the seat from an old pony and trap.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Kirstie and Phil help Niki and Rob, who want a home that has space for a future family, and Georgie and Louis, who hope to cut down their commute by moving to London.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Matt reveals a big makeover project for the farm, but before they can start building, there's demolition to be done. And the farm celebrates a new addition.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Spain, rookie hoteliers Jo and Ken hope to boost their business, and mum and daughter Becky and Kay start a top-to-bottom renovation of a B&B.
Bev and Errol can't wait to buy a Spanish holiday home in Mar Menor. Jasmine Harman shows them what their £65k can buy.

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What
programmes.show_description: Happened Next?: How are Kate and Wayne are getting on in their perfect villa in Murcia? And what has Keith done to his French fancy in Tarn-et-Garonne?
Nat and Dave are ready to buy a holiday home on Lanzarote, a place they visit twice a year. Jean tries to find their perfect property on the island they love so much.
Cardiff. The first host, dog-loving shop manager Nicola, serves up prawn cocktail, meat-free lasagne, doggy surprises and unusual entertainment. (Ep1/5)
Cardiff. King of the name-droppers Jerry Lockett hosts at his cousin's mansion, where he serves up dollops of food and a side order of celebrity. (Ep2/5)
Cardiff. Extremely competitive army wife Rachael Smith hosts the third night. She isn't used to losing, and things might get ugly if she doesn't win the prize! (Ep3/5)
Cardiff. Flamboyant costume designer Sam hosts a Mexican night full of sizzle and spice. But a game makes everyone cry... are they tears of joy, sorrow or pain? (Ep4/5)
Cardiff. Childminder Andrea Grainger plans a warm welcome, a traditional Welsh menu and some fun and games on the last night. (Ep5/5)

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back at lofty ambitions in Walthamstow in East London, where the Clayton-Fisher family totally transformed their dark and dingy attic.
A couple create an ambitious floating home, George and Will make a magical vista in their garden flat, and two siblings build their own mobile homes away from home.

programmes.show_name: Help! I Bought It at Auction...
programmes.show_description: ...with Sarah Beeny: Brave auction buyers convert derelict digs into diamond homes. Alison and Michael take on a pitfall palace. Jules and Al tackle a creaky Cornwall B&B. (Ep1)
Kazal and Eleena are ditching Dubai and moving back home to Solihull to be close to family. Can they overcome building blunders to create the ultimate hosting hub?
The Goggleboxers have been watching Married at First Sight Australia, Pangolin: Kulu's Journey, The Honesty Box, Silence Is Golden and Good Luck To You, Leo Grande.
Witnesses, survivors and front-line heroes recall the first days of the Chernobyl disaster of April 1986. - featuring rare archive footage of the world's worst ever nuclear accident.
